bead.title: bead-lifecycle: guard readiness downgrade against operator-promoted beads
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Readiness terminal handling unconditionally parks beads at status=proposed when an ambiguous outcome is reached. When an operator has already promoted the same bead back to open, the next readiness pass downgrades it again, producing a proposed->open->proposed loop and interrupting forward progress.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 calls the terminal readiness handler that sets status=proposed without consulting the bead transition history; there is no check for a prior operator-driven proposed->open transition.

PROPOSED FIX
Before the terminal downgrade, inspect the bead's transition audit log for the most recent status change. If the latest transition is proposed->open and was made by an operator (not the agent), skip the downgrade and instead record an observable readiness warning (handled by sibling observability bead). Agent-driven beads in proposed continue to receive terminal handling as today.

NON-SCOPE
- Updating FEAT-010/ADR-023 policy text (covered by sibling spec bead).
- Extending intake.blocked payload fields (covered by sibling observability bead).
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: this epic. Soft dependency on the spec bead for normative reference; can land in parallel.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestReadinessPreservesOperatorPromotedOpen asserts that a bead with a recent operator-driven proposed->open transition remains status=open after an ambiguous readiness terminal outcome.
2. TestReadinessStillDowngradesAgentProposed asserts that a bead in status=proposed with no operator promotion still receives terminal downgrade handling.
3. TestReadinessLoopBoundedAfterOperatorPromotion runs readiness twice over the same operator-promoted bead and asserts zero new status transitions are written on the second pass.
4. TestReadinessHonorsOperatorAcceptanceAcrossSessions asserts the operator-acceptance signal is read from the persisted transition log, not in-memory state, so it survives process restart.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passing.
